patches.summary: Add persistent DoorDash browser-session transport support with auth status/login/logout commands.
patches.reason: The generated sniffed GraphQL CLI had raw endpoints but no cookie jar, CSRF handling, browser-context headers, or safe session import flow for live DoorDash use.
patches.validated_outcome: PER-34 validation passed on 2026-05-13T23:02Z: go test ./... and make build-all passed using /home/hermes/.local/bin/go (go1.26.3); built CLI doctor reports api=reachable after preserving HTTP/2 support; auth status prints safe metadata only and no cookie values.

patches.summary: Add a curated cart place command and hard-gate raw createOrderFromCart order placement.
patches.reason: DoorDash order placement can charge bricenice17's card, so live requests must be disabled by default and require explicit bricenice17 approval plus an exact confirmation phrase; --agent/--yes must not bypass this.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passed; cart place --dry-run sends no request; cart place and graphql create-create-order-from-cart both exit 2 without all live-order gates

patches.summary: Register all 19 curated DoorDash GraphQL operations in the generated CLI skeleton.
patches.reason: The active generated skeleton only exposed the 8 browser-sniffed operations even though spec.yaml and the curated manifest already contained 19 operations; Phase 2 needs the clean skeleton to match the formal spec.
patches.validated_outcome: PER-33 validation passed on 2026-05-13T22:59:57Z: make test and make build-all passed using /home/hermes/.local/bin/go (go1.26.3); agent-context reports entry_count=19/api_entry_count=19; graphql --help lists 19 create-* commands; spec and reference checksums match PROVENANCE.md.

patches.summary: Add a curated cart preview command for checkout and optional fee-tally review without order placement.
patches.reason: bricenice17 needs a safe pre-order view of restaurant, items, delivery address, payment method, tip, fees, and total; the command must never call createOrderFromCart.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passed; go build ./cmd/doordash-pp-cli passed; cart preview --dry-run --agent with checkout and fee variables printed POST previews for /graphql/checkout and /graphql/totalFeeTally, returned order_placement=not_called, and did not call createOrderFromCart.

patches.summary: Add curated read-only commands and checkout preview helpers for safe DoorDash agent use.
patches.reason: Agents need higher-level read-only search/menu/item/recent-order/address/payment flows and a fee/checkout preview that cannot place an order; raw GraphQL commands are too easy to misuse.
patches.validated_outcome: go test -vet=off ./internal/cli -run 'TestReadOnlyCommandsRegistered|TestParse' passed; ./scripts/smoke-doordash-cli.sh passed; installed CLI which resolves 'search restaurants' and 'checkout preview'

patches.summary: Keep active doordash-pp-cli PATH locations on the Node/CycleTLS wrapper and shared DoorDash session.
patches.reason: Paperclip/Herman profile HOME and PATH ordering exposed stale generated Go binaries, causing DoorDash 403s and command-surface mismatches for browser-facing live calls.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T05:07Z: active command doctor reports authenticated=true/csrf_present=true; live read-only smoke passes for search/menu/item-options/convenience/orders/addresses/payment/cart; order place rejects without ALLOW_DOORDASH_ORDERING=1.

patches.summary: Expose addCartItemV2 order-item IDs and store-scoped cart show for safer live cart debugging.
patches.reason: The previous controlled live cart test returned a cart ID but no visible cart; the next approved test needs the exact added item ID when DoorDash returns it and a read-only store-scoped listCarts check before any removal/cleanup attempt.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T05:11Z: node --check passed for dist/api/cart.js and dist/cli.js; active CLI help shows cart show --store-id; cart add --dry-run returns safely; cart show --store-id 2418408 returns a list; doctor remains authenticated/csrf_present with consumer_id_present=false; cart add without --yes and order place without ALLOW_DOORDASH_ORDERING=1 both reject. No live mutation/order attempted.

patches.summary: Synchronize repo docs with the active Node/CycleTLS wrapper command surface.
patches.reason: AGENTS.md, README, SKILL, and status docs referenced generated-only helpers such as agent-context/which and older cart/auth command names; Paperclip agents should follow the installed wrapper runtime truth.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T06:38Z: go test -vet=off ./... passed; Go CLI/MCP builds passed; node --check passed for dist/cli.js, dist/api/cart.js, and dist/checkout-preview.js; active wrapper doctor/help passed; live read-only smoke passed; cart add/remove dry-runs returned no-mutation previews; cart add/remove without --yes rejected; order place without ALLOW_DOORDASH_ORDERING=1 rejected; session dir/file modes are 0700/0600.

patches.summary: Verify live guarded cart add/remove and checkout preview with the refreshed full DoorDash session.
patches.reason: The previous live-cart blocker was a lite-guest session with consumer_id_present=false; current doctor reports consumer_id_present=true, so Phase 5/6 could be safely revalidated without order placement.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T11:52Z: active wrapper doctor/help passed; session dir/file modes 0700/0600; go test -vet=off ./... passed; Go CLI/MCP builds passed; live read-only smoke passed; dry-run/no-yes/order gates passed; guarded Pizza Hut Garlic Dip add/remove returned cart/item IDs and final store-scoped cart count 0; checkout preview returned safety.placesOrder=false, fees/totals present, and final cleanup verified. Raw preview containing address/payment metadata was deleted after sanitizing. 2026-05-14T11:56Z recheck: absolute installed wrapper doctor/help passed with authenticated=true, csrf_present=true, consumer_id_present=true; Go test/build passed; live read-only smoke passed; cart add --dry-run, cart add without --yes rejection, and order place without ALLOW_DOORDASH_ORDERING rejection all passed without live order placement.

patches.summary: Polish active Node/CycleTLS wrapper read-only answers for recent orders, ranked Thai stores, and cheapest chicken parmesan menu search.
patches.reason: bricenice17’s first real read-only test exposed that order items were not first-class, store rankings lacked enriched rating/delivery fields, and cheapest menu item answers required ad-hoc probing instead of a direct command.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-14T15:53Z: npm run build passed using /home/hermes/.local/bin/npm; generated Go skeleton checks passed with /home/hermes/.local/bin/go test -vet=off ./... plus go build for both CLI and MCP commands; active installed wrapper doctor/help passed with authenticated=true, csrf_present=true, consumer_id_present=true; orders recent --limit 2 --json returned itemDetails; stores rank Thai --limit 5 --json returned enriched ranked stores with normalized rating displays; items search "chicken parmesan" --store-query "italian" --stores 5 --limit 10 --json returned dinner_or_pasta and sub_or_sandwich cheapest buckets. No cart/checkout/order mutation commands were run.

patches.summary: Reprint the generated Go skeleton with Printing Press 4.6.1 and merge safe template/test updates while preserving DoorDash-specific runtime patches.
patches.reason: Printing Press 4.6.1 tightened generator output, MCP shellout safety tests, API body truncation behavior, usage-error classification, and manifest printer attribution. The DoorDash repo remains a hybrid: active Node/CycleTLS runtime plus generated Go PP reference tree.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passed in generated-cli after restoring DoorDash-specific session/cart/read patches and tightening PP 4.6.1 test expectations.

patches.summary: Remove stale unregistered tail command and unused generated helper functions from the Go reference tree.
patches.reason: Dogfood flagged the generated tail command as unregistered and the pagination/envelope helpers as dead code; these paths are not used by the DoorDash live GraphQL/cart command surface and keeping them adds submit noise.
patches.validated_outcome: 2026-05-23: generated-cli go test ./..., go build ./..., go vet ./..., and printing-press publish validate all passed; dogfood now reports no dead functions and all defined commands registered, with only the existing sync-pipeline heuristic warning remaining.

patches.summary: Fix public submit review issues for cart preview query resolution, deterministic cache keys, and generic approval wording.
patches.reason: Greptile and library CI flagged production-path issues after the first submission: cart preview sent query file names as GraphQL documents, GET cache keys depended on map iteration order, and a live-order flag description embedded a maintainer handle.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and scoped govulncheck passed before updating PR #773.

patches.summary: Apply live-order safety gates to the raw createOrderFromCart GraphQL command and update public README examples for the generated command surface.
patches.reason: Greptile flagged that cart place was gated but graphql create-create-order-from-cart could still post to the order endpoint directly; the newer Printing Press validator also rejected stale Node-wrapper README command examples.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and Printing Press 4.12 publish validate passed locally before updating PR #773.

patches.summary: Remove createOrderFromCart from the generic MCP API tools and require agents to use guarded Cobra-mirrored commands.
patches.reason: Greptile flagged that the direct MCP tool could place a live DoorDash order without the CLI live-order gates and was marked non-destructive.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... and scoped govulncheck passed before updating PR #784.

patches.summary: Parse MCP GraphQL variables strings into JSON objects before posting typed endpoint requests.
patches.reason: Greptile flagged that typed MCP GraphQL tools forwarded variables as a JSON string, causing parameterized GraphQL calls to fail because the HTTP body requires variables to be an object.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passed in library/commerce/doordash before updating PR #784.

patches.summary: Hide live order placement confirmation flags from the cobratree MCP schema.
patches.reason: Greptile flagged that cart_place exposed the exact live-order gate flags as MCP parameters, letting an agent satisfy all confirmation gates through one structured MCP call.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passed in library/commerce/doordash before updating PR #784.
